pr.title: Integration PR #144 â€” Self-Model Gap Analysis v1
pr.description:
## Summary Builds the next integration milestone on top of the self-model populated in PR #143 (#33). Where PR #143 made Axiom answer *positive* structural questions ("what depends on X?"), this PR adds executable **negative discovery**: Axiom reads its own populated self-model and emits a ranked, structured **integration backlog** of what is missing, disconnected, unused, or unexplained. This is an **analyzer/adapter only** â€” no new framework, object model, graph, registry, or evidence system, and no broad refactor. It reuses the existing `capability_knowledge_graph` report as input and existing `code-inventory` / `command_registry` / `capability_summary` data as supporting evidence, returning plain `dict` findings (mirrors the `self_model.py` adapter from PR #143). ### What it does `SelfModelGapAnalyzer(report, *, module_classes=None, documented_modules=None, chain_stages=None).analyze()` classifies all 9 required gap categories from the graph's nodes/edges/`orphan_node_ids`: ``` isolated_modules Â· unconsumed_modules Â· no_outgoing_dependency_modules command_modules_with_low_connectivity Â· declared_but_unwired_chains artifact_or_evidence_producers_without_consumers missing_purpose_or_layer_candidates Â· disconnected_framework_families recommended_integration_candidates (ranked roll-up) ``` Each gap carries the full required backlog shape: `gap_id, gap_type, affected_modules, evidence, why_it_matters, existing_capabilities_to_reuse, proposed_smallest_fix, expected_new_behavior, validation_strategy, priority`. Output is deterministic (sorted module lists, stable `TYPE-NNN` ids, fixed `(-score, gap_type, gap_id)` ordering). Ranking weights reflect the directive's criteria â€” chain blockers and evidenceâ†’promotion gaps score highest. **Declared-but-unwired chain detection** walks `ExecutionPlan â†’ ExecutionStep â†’ ExecutionAttempt â†’ ExecutionResult â†’ ExecutionArtifact â†’ ExecutionReport` and flags any stage pair with no supporting import edge in either direction â€” using repository evidence, not hard-coded conclusions. Each stage resolves to whichever candidate module node is actually present; a stage whose module is absent is flagged distinctly. ### New CLI command (read-only, registered in `command_registry`) ``` axiom self-model-gap-analysis --graph [--repo-root

] \ [--artifacts-root

] [--export ] [--json-output] ``` `--export foo.md` writes the Markdown backlog to `foo.md` and the JSON backlog to the sibling `foo.json`. ### Negative discovery, executed against the real self-model Building the self-model (158 modules / 275 edges / 9 isolated) and running the analyzer produced **16 gaps**, e.g. all 5 execution-chain transitions flagged unwired â€” and `ExecutionArtifact â†’ ExecutionReport` correctly flagged because `execution_report.py` is not on `main` (PR #142 unmerged), proving the findings are generated from real repository evidence rather than restated from the prior Self-Discovery report. ## Validation - Targeted `tests/test_self_model_gap_analysis.py` â†’ **21 passed** (fixtures for isolated/connected/command-only/producer modules, wired vs unwired chains, JSON + Markdown output, deterministic ordering, required-fields, >1 failure class). - `tests/test_command_registry.py` + `tests/test_self_model.py` + targeted â†’ **60 passed**. - Full `poetry run pytest -q` â†’ **4950 passed, 1 skipped** (preexisting skip). - `ruff check src/ tests/` â†’ clean. - CLI smoke: `self-model-build` â†’ `self-model-gap-analysis` (console + `--json-output` valid JSON + `--export` writing `.md` + `.json`). ## What did not change No execution/graph/relationship engines were modified; no Revit live path touched; 2024 baseline unaffected. Introspection-only. Does not implement M2/M3/M4/M5 â€” it produces the ranked candidates used to *choose* the next integration PR. ## Strategic fit Strengthens the verification factory's self-understanding: turns failure discovery into executable platform behavior and surfaces exactly where evidence is not feeding trust/promotion and where the execution chain is not yet wired end-to-end. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7 Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Integration PR #144 â€” Self-Model Gap Analysis v1

Add executable negative-discovery on top of the populated self-model:
a SelfModelGapAnalyzer that reads the existing capability-knowledge-graph
report (plus existing command-registry / capability-summary metadata) and
emits a ranked, structured integration backlog classifying 9 gap categories,
including declared-but-unwired execution-chain detection. Adds one read-only
CLI command (self-model-gap-analysis) and registers it. No new framework.

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
